title: Getting random 403 Forbidden errors on the dashboard
body: I keep getting 403 errors when I try to access the TaskFlow dashboard. It's not every time — sometimes it loads fine, sometimes I get a 403 page. It seems totally random. My permissions haven't changed and other people on my team say they're seeing it too. Started happening a couple days ago.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for reporting this — intermittent 403s affecting multiple people definitely sounds like something worth digging into.

Can you walk me through what a typical session looks like when this happens? For example, are you hitting the 403 right when you first load the dashboard, or does it come up after you've been using it for a while or performing a specific action? And when you say it started a couple days ago — do you recall if anything changed around that time, like a deployment, a configuration update, or even something like a team membership change?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: It happens right away when I try to load the dashboard — like I'll click to go to the dashboard and sometimes I just immediately get a 403. If I refresh a couple times it usually works. There's no specific action that triggers it, it's pretty random. As for what changed, I was actually just given a new role — I got switched to the 'analyst' role a few days ago. I'm not sure if that's related though, I figured it was maybe network issues or something. My coworkers who also have the analyst role seem to be having the same problem.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
That's a really helpful detail about the role change — I think that might be more connected than you'd expect.

Can you tell me more about this 'analyst' role? Is it a role that's been around for a while and other people already had, or was it recently created? And do you know if anyone on your team who *doesn't* have the analyst role is also seeing these 403s, or is it only folks who were switched to that role?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: I think the analyst role is pretty new — it was just added recently, maybe around the same time the problems started actually. As far as I know, the people having issues are all ones who got the analyst role. I haven't heard complaints from anyone who's still on the regular roles like editor or admin. So yeah, it might be related now that you mention it.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
This is really starting to paint a clear picture — the analyst role timing lines up too well to be coincidence.

The part that's most interesting to me now is the intermittency. If the role simply didn't have dashboard access, you'd be blocked every time — but instead it works sometimes and fails other times. That pattern usually points to something like caching or inconsistent state across servers.

When it fails and you refresh to get it working, does it tend to stay working for the rest of that session? Or can it flip back to a 403 again mid-session? And have you noticed any pattern at all — like it's worse at certain times of day, or right after logging in, or perhaps after being idle for a while?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: Hmm, good question. It doesn't really stay working consistently — like I can load the dashboard fine, then click to another page and get a 403 again, then refresh and it's fine. It kind of flips back and forth. I haven't noticed a time-of-day pattern really. If I had to guess I'd say it works maybe like 2 out of 3 times? But I haven't kept exact track. It just feels random to me. I don't think it's related to being idle or anything like that, it happens even when I'm actively clicking around.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"Intermittent 403 Forbidden errors for users with the new 'analyst' role","problem":"Users assigned the recently-created 'analyst' role are experiencing intermittent 403 Forbidden errors across the application. The errors occur on initial page loads and during active navigation, with roughly a 1-in-3 failure rate. Users on established roles (editor, admin) are unaffected. The issue began around the same time the analyst role was introduced.","root_cause_hypothesis":"The new 'analyst' role's permissions are not consistently available across all application server instances or cache nodes. This is likely caused by one of: (1) a stale or inconsistently-replicated permission/role cache across multiple app servers behind a load balancer, (2) the role definition or its permission grants not being fully propagated to all instances after creation, or (3) a race condition in the authorization check where the role lookup intermittently fails to find the new role's grants and defaults to deny.","reproduction_steps":["Assign a user the 'analyst' role","Attempt to load the TaskFlow dashboard repeatedly (10-15 times)","Observe that approximately 1 in 3 requests returns a 403 Forbidden","Note that refreshing after a 403 often succeeds, and subsequent navigation can produce another 403"],"environment":"Affects multiple users with the 'analyst' role. No specific browser, OS, or network dependency identified — the issue appears server-side. Likely a multi-instance deployment behind a load balancer.","severity":"high","impact":"All users assigned the new 'analyst' role are intermittently locked out of the dashboard and other pages. This blocks their workflow roughly one-third of the time and affects the entire analyst user group.","recommended_fix":"1. Inspect the authorization/permission layer for how role grants are cached and distributed across app server instances. 2. Check whether the 'analyst' role's permission entries were fully committed and replicated (e.g., database replication lag, in-memory cache not invalidated on all nodes). 3. Compare the permission resolution path for the 'analyst' role vs established roles like 'editor' — look for missing cache warm-up or a fallback-to-deny when a role lookup misses. 4. If a distributed cache (Redis, Memcached) is used for permissions, verify that the analyst role's grants are present in all cache nodes.","proposed_test_case":"Write an integration test that creates a new role with dashboard permissions, assigns it to a test user, then makes 50 sequential authenticated requests to the dashboard endpoint across multiple app instances (or with cache clearing between requests). Assert that all 50 requests return 200, not 403. This validates that new roles are immediately and consistently authorized.","information_gaps":["Exact deployment topology (number of app server instances, load balancer type)","Which caching layer is used for authorization (in-memory, Redis, database query cache)","Whether the analyst role was added via migration, admin UI, or API — and whether a cache flush or deployment accompanied it","Server-side logs showing which authorization check is returning 403"]}
